architecture.frontend: Next.js 14 (App Router) + Tailwind CSS, deployed on Vercel
architecture.backend: Single Next.js Route Handler at app/api/signup/route.ts (no separate Node service this cycle)
architecture.database: PostgreSQL via Drizzle ORM, hosted on Neon (free tier)
architecture.infrastructure: Vercel (frontend + API route, automatic SSL + global CDN) + Neon Postgres + Cloudflare for the apex domain

apiContracts.description: Public endpoint called by the landing page form to capture an email signup. Idempotent on (email): a duplicate submission returns status: 'duplicate' with the original id rather than a 409.

fields: id: uuid primary key default gen_random_uuid()
created_at: timestamptz not null default now()

risks: security: /api/signup is intentionally public; needs IP-based rate limiting (e.g. 5 req/min) before launch — flag for security review when the handler task lands
compliance: signups holds PII (email); add a privacy notice on the form and confirm Neon's data residency before launch
scaling: not relevant this cycle (validate phase, < 10k rows expected)
